steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence. The sentence names and defines the linguistic phenomenon just illustrated by the pit'iin' / pi-pit'in' examples, then the next sentence explains how that phenomenon works in this specific case. The underlined sentence sits between examples and explanation — its job is to supply the definition that connects them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It explains where the language discussed in the text is from.': The passage's first sentence already establishes that Nimipuutimt is from the interior Northwest. The underlined sentence says nothing about geographic origin. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It contrasts the language discussed in the text with other languages.': No other language is mentioned anywhere in the passage. The underlined sentence makes no comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It provides English translations of the Nimipuutimt words discussed in the text.': Translations of pit'iin' and pi-pit'in' appear in the opening sentence, not in the underlined sentence. The underlined sentence defines reduplication, not vocabulary. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It provides a general definition of the phenomenon discussed in the text.': The underlined sentence defines reduplication — "an element of a root word is repeated, sometimes with modification, within another word related to the root word" — giving the reader the general rule behind the specific examples. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
